title: fraxinus caroliniana f. pubescens
intro: Fraxinus caroliniana f. pubescens, also known as the Carolina ash, is a lesser-known variety of the Fraxinus caroliniana tree, characterized by its pubescent or hairy leaves. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its high tannin and phenolic acid content. The health benefits of Fraxinus caroliniana f. pubescens include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional remedies for skin conditions and gastrointestinal issues.
